title: Oreogrammitis congener
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis congener belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and is a species of fern in the genus Oreogrammitis. The Polypodiaceae family is a large and diverse group of ferns, commonly known as polypody ferns. Oreogrammitis congener is native to New Caledonia, a region in the Pacific Ocean. This species is part of the larger class Polypodiopsida, which includes all the ferns. Oreogrammitis congener is also classified as a member of the subclass Polypodiidae.

intro: Oreogrammitis congener, also known as the "Ceylon fern", is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis congener include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory infections.
